title: Leveraging GPU-acceleration in bixverse single cell
text: vignette walks standard single cell analysis PBMC3k data set, demonstrating bixverse.gpu can used drop-replacement CPU-based neighbour search bixverse. Everything including PCA identical PBMC vignette main package - GPU-specific bits come play neighbour search step. core idea GPU methods hardware agnostic, hence, usage CubeCL WGPU backend make code run different GPUs. Also, time, quite overkill outside massive data sets usually worth (always want exhaustive, exact kNNs). idea bixverse code looks quite unusual single cell, please read . bixverse.gpu provides three GPU-accelerated kNN algorithms via wgpu backend: Exhaustive: Exact brute-force search. Precise, scales quadratically. Best suited smaller data sets need exact neighbours. IVF: Inverted file index. Partitions embedding space Voronoi cells probes subset query time. Good throughput larger data sets minor precision trade-. CAGRA: Builds pruned navigational graph via NNDescent. Excellent recall high-dimensional embeddings large data sets. Supports two retrieval modes: direct extraction NNDescent graph (faster, slightly less precise) beam search pruned graph (slower, higher recall). exhaustive IVF methods exposed find_neighbours_gpu_sc(), whilst CAGRA generic, find_neighbours_cagra_sc(), owing distinct parameter set.
code: library(bixverse) library(bixverse.gpu) library(ggplot2) library(data.table)

title: Leveraging GPU-acceleration in bixverse single cell
text: Let’s load data know bixverse.
code: pbmc3k_path <- bixverse:::download_pbmc3k()  tempdir_pbmc <- tempdir()  sc_object <- SingleCells(dir_data = tempdir_pbmc)  mtx_io_params <- get_cell_ranger_params(pbmc3k_path)  sc_object <- load_mtx(   object = sc_object,   sc_mtx_io_param = mtx_io_params,   streaming = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Loading observations data from flat file into the DuckDB. #> Loading variable data from flat file into the DuckDB.  var <- get_sc_var(sc_object)  setnames_sc(   object = sc_object,   table = "var",   old = "column1",   new = "gene_symbol" )  var <- get_sc_var(sc_object)  ensembl_to_symbol <- setNames(var$gene_symbol, var$gene_id) symbol_to_ensembl <- setNames(var$gene_id, var$gene_symbol)

title: Leveraging GPU-acceleration in bixverse single cell
text: QC per usual… Outlier detection: Set labels use.
code: gs_of_interest <- list(   MT = var[grepl("^MT-", gene_symbol), gene_id],   Ribo = var[grepl("^RPS|^RPL", gene_symbol), gene_id] )  sc_object <- gene_set_proportions_sc(   sc_object,   gs_of_interest,   streaming = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE ) qc_df <- sc_object[[c("cell_id", "lib_size", "nnz", "MT")]]  metrics <- list(   log10_lib_size = log10(qc_df$lib_size),   log10_nnz = log10(qc_df$nnz),   MT = qc_df$MT )  directions <- c(   log10_lib_size = "twosided",   log10_nnz = "twosided",   MT = "above" )  qc <- run_cell_qc(metrics, directions, threshold = 3) sc_object[["outlier"]] <- qc$combined  cells_to_keep <- qc_df[!qc$combined, cell_id]  sc_object <- set_cells_to_keep(sc_object, cells_to_keep)

title: Leveraging GPU-acceleration in bixverse single cell
text: Nothing GPU-specific : HVG selection PCA identical CPU pipeline. However, something important see… way GPU code written, number dimensions
code: sc_object <- find_hvg_sc(   object = sc_object,   hvg_no = 2000L,   .verbose = TRUE )  sc_object <- calculate_pca_sc(   object = sc_object,   no_pcs = 32L,   sparse_svd = TRUE ) #> Using sparse SVD solving on scaled data on 2000 HVG.

title: Leveraging GPU-acceleration in bixverse single cell
text: bixverse.gpu comes . show three methods. practice pick one - one depends size data much care exact neighbours versus speed. data set small PBMC3k, exhaustive search GPU straightforward option. might actually slower CPU search tiny data set due moving data RAM VRAM (Apple Silicon) GPU kernel launch overhead. shows works… becomes however much faster data GPU churn . IVF becomes worthwhile larger data sets. key tuning knobs nlist (number Voronoi partitions) nprobe (many partitions search query time). PBMC3k defaults perfectly fine. CAGRA builds navigational graph via NNDescent prunes efficient beam search. Setting extract_knn = TRUE skips beam search pulls kNN directly NNDescent graph, faster slightly less precise. extract_knn = FALSE, function runs beam search pruned CAGRA graph. slower yields higher recall, can matter larger, higher-dimensional data. point , SingleCells object carries kNN sNN graph exactly call find_neighbours_sc (see bixverse function) - downstream methods (clustering, UMAP, marker detection, etc.) work without modification.
code: sc_exhaustive <- find_neighbours_gpu_sc(   object = sc_object,   gpu_method = "exhaustive",   k = 15L,   dist_metric = "cosine",   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with exhaustive method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph. sc_ivf <- find_neighbours_gpu_sc(   object = sc_object,   gpu_method = "ivf",   ivf_params = params_sc_ivf(k = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with ivf method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph. sc_cagra_extract <- find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object = sc_object,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(k_query = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   extract_knn = TRUE,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with CAGRA method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph. sc_cagra_beam <- find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object = sc_object,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(k_query = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   extract_knn = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with CAGRA method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph.

code: sc_ivf <- find_neighbours_gpu_sc(   object = sc_object,   gpu_method = "ivf",   ivf_params = params_sc_ivf(k = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with ivf method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph.

Setting extract_knn = TRUE skips beam search pulls kNN directly NNDescent graph, faster slightly less precise. extract_knn = FALSE, function runs beam search pruned CAGRA graph. slower yields higher recall, can matter larger, higher-dimensional data. point , SingleCells object carries kNN sNN graph exactly call find_neighbours_sc (see bixverse function) - downstream methods (clustering, UMAP, marker detection, etc.) work without modification.
code: sc_cagra_extract <- find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object = sc_object,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(k_query = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   extract_knn = TRUE,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with CAGRA method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph. sc_cagra_beam <- find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object = sc_object,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(k_query = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   extract_knn = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Generating GPU kNN data with CAGRA method. #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph.

what: Find CAGRA GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells — find_neighbours_cagra_sc
title: Find CAGRA GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells — find_neighbours_cagra_sc
text: function generates kNN data using CAGRA (CUDA-Accelerated Graph Retrieval Approximation) algorithm wgpu backend via bixverse.gpu package. CAGRA first builds dense NNDescent graph, prunes sparser navigational graph optimised beam-search traversal. Two retrieval modes available: direct extraction NNDescent graph (extract_knn = TRUE), faster slightly less precise, beam search pruned CAGRA graph (extract_knn = FALSE), slower yields higher recall. CAGRA tends perform well high-dimensional embeddings large data sets. Subsequently, kNN data used generate sNN igraph downstream clustering. find_neighbours_gpu_sc(), function lives separate package users without GPU hardware required install GPU dependencies.

title: Find CAGRA GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells — find_neighbours_cagra_sc
code: find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object,   embd_to_use = "pca",   no_embd_to_use = NULL,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(),   extract_knn = TRUE,   snn_params = params_sc_neighbours(),   seed = 42L,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Find CAGRA GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells — find_neighbours_cagra_sc
text: object SingleCells class. embd_to_use String. embedding use. Whichever choose, needs part object. no_embd_to_use Optional integer. Number embedding dimensions use. NULL used. cagra_params List. Output params_sc_cagra(). list following items: k_query - Integer. Number nearest neighbours return final result. ann_dist - String. Distance metric; one c("euclidean", "cosine"). k - Optional integer. Final node degree pruned CAGRA navigational graph. Controls sparsity search graph; higher values improve recall increase memory usage. NULL, defaults 30. k_build - Optional integer. Number neighbours NNDescent build phase CAGRA pruning. NULL, defaults 1.5 * k. refine_sweeps - Integer. Number refinement sweeps graph construction. sweeps improve graph quality cost build time. max_iters - Optional integer. Maximum iterations NNDescent rounds. NULL, determined automatically. n_trees - Optional integer. Number trees initial GPU-accelerated random projection forest used seed NNDescent. NULL, determined automatically. delta - Numeric. Early-stopping criterion NNDescent; iterations terminate fewer delta fraction neighbours change. rho - Optional numeric. Sampling rate NNDescent iterations. Lower values speed construction cost graph quality. NULL, determined automatically. beam_width - Optional integer. Beam width graph search. Larger beams improve recall slow querying. NULL, determined automatically. max_beam_iters - Optional integer. Maximum beam search iterations. NULL, determined automatically. n_entry_points - Optional integer. Number entry points CAGRA graph. NULL, determined automatically. extract_knn Logical. TRUE, extracts kNN graph directly NNDescent result (faster, slightly lower precision). FALSE, runs beam search pruned CAGRA graph (slower, higher precision). snn_params List. Output bixverse::params_sc_neighbours(). Controls sNN graph construction. relevant items : full_snn - Boolean. Whether generate edges cells rather neighbours. pruning - Numeric. Weights threshold set 0 sNN graph. snn_similarity - String. One c("rank", "jaccard"). Defines sNN edge weights calculated. seed Integer. reproducibility. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity.

title: Find CAGRA GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells — find_neighbours_cagra_sc
text: object added kNN matrix sNN graph.

what: Find GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells (exhaustive / IVF) — find_neighbours_gpu_sc
title: Find GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells (exhaustive / IVF) — find_neighbours_gpu_sc
text: function generates kNN data using GPU-accelerated algorithms via bixverse.gpu package. Two methods available: "exhaustive" performs exact brute-force search GPU, precise scales quadratically; "ivf" builds inverted file index partitions embedding space Voronoi cells probes subset query time, trading small amount precision considerably faster search larger data sets. Subsequently, kNN data used generate sNN igraph downstream clustering. function lives separate package CPU-based bixverse::find_neighbours_sc() users without GPU hardware need install GPU dependencies.

title: Find GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells (exhaustive / IVF) — find_neighbours_gpu_sc
code: find_neighbours_gpu_sc(   object,   embd_to_use = "pca",   no_embd_to_use = NULL,   gpu_method = c("exhaustive", "ivf"),   ivf_params = params_sc_ivf(),   k = 15L,   dist_metric = "cosine",   snn_params = params_sc_neighbours(),   seed = 42L,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Find GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells (exhaustive / IVF) — find_neighbours_gpu_sc
text: object SingleCells class. embd_to_use String. embedding use. Whichever choose, needs part object. no_embd_to_use Optional integer. Number embedding dimensions use. NULL used. gpu_method String. One c("exhaustive", "ivf"). "exhaustive" computes exact nearest neighbours via brute-force GPU. "ivf" builds inverted file index approximate search. ivf_params List. Output params_sc_ivf(). used gpu_method = "ivf". list following items: k - Integer. Number nearest neighbours identify. ann_dist - String. Distance metric; one c("euclidean", "cosine"). nlist - Optional integer. Number clusters partition index . Controls granularity Voronoi partitioning. NULL, defaults sqrt(n) Rust side. nprobe - Optional integer. Number clusters probe query time. Higher values improve recall cost speed. NULL, defaults sqrt(nlist). nquery - Optional integer. Number query vectors processed per GPU batch. NULL, defaults 100,000. max_iters - Optional integer. Maximum k-means iterations index construction. NULL, defaults 30. seed - Integer. Seed k-means initialisation. k Integer. Number neighbours. used gpu_method = "exhaustive". dist_metric String. One c("euclidean", "cosine"). used gpu_method = "exhaustive". snn_params List. Output bixverse::params_sc_neighbours(). Controls sNN graph construction. relevant items : full_snn - Boolean. Whether generate edges cells rather neighbours. pruning - Numeric. Weights threshold set 0 sNN graph. snn_similarity - String. One c("rank", "jaccard"). Defines sNN edge weights calculated. seed Integer. reproducibility. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity.

title: Find GPU-accelerated neighbours for single cells (exhaustive / IVF) — find_neighbours_gpu_sc

code: params_sc_cagra(   k_query = 15L,   ann_dist = "cosine",   k = NULL,   k_build = NULL,   refine_sweeps = 0L,   max_iters = NULL,   n_trees = NULL,   delta = 0.001,   rho = NULL,   beam_width = NULL,   max_beam_iters = NULL,   n_entry_points = NULL )

title: Default parameters for CAGRA-style kNN search — params_sc_cagra
text: k_query Integer. Number neighbours identify. ann_dist Character. Distance metric use. One "euclidean" "cosine". k Optional integer. Final node degree CAGRA navigational graph. NULL, defaults 30 Rust side. k_build Optional integer. Number k-neighbours NNDescent build phase CAGRA pruning. NULL, defaults 1.5 * k Rust side. refine_sweeps Integer. Number refinement sweeps graph generation. max_iters Optional integer. Maximum iterations NNDescent rounds. NULL, determined automatically. n_trees Optional integer. Number trees use initial GPU-accelerated forest. NULL, determined automatically. delta Numeric. Termination criterion NNDescent iterations. rho Optional numeric. Sampling rate NNDescent iterations. NULL, determined automatically. beam_width Optional integer. Beam width querying. NULL, determined automatically. max_beam_iters Optional integer. Maximum beam iterations. NULL, determined automatically. n_entry_points Optional integer. Number entry points graph. NULL, determined automatically.

what: Generate a CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated kNN graph — rs_cagra_gpu_knn
title: Generate a CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated kNN graph — rs_cagra_gpu_knn
text: Builds kNN graph embedding matrix using CAGRA algorithm wgpu backend. Supports two retrieval modes: direct extraction NNDescent graph, beam search pruned CAGRA graph.

title: Generate a CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated kNN graph — rs_cagra_gpu_knn
code: rs_cagra_gpu_knn(embd, cagra_params, extract_knn, seed, verbose)

title: Generate a CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated kNN graph — rs_cagra_gpu_knn
text: embd Numeric matrix embeddings, cells x features. cagra_params named list parameters, see params_sc_cagra() extract_knn Logical. TRUE, extracts kNN graph directly NNDescent result (faster, slightly lower precision). FALSE, runs beam search pruned CAGRA graph (slower, higher precision). seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages.

title: Generate a CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated kNN graph — rs_cagra_gpu_knn
text: named list : indices - Integer matrix shape cells x k_query 0-based neighbour indices. dist - Numeric matrix shape cells x k_query distances neighbours. dist_metric - Character. distance metric used.
